# HypeRank setup checklist

Things only you can do (accounts, DNS, secrets, OAuth). Ordered **do first → do later**.

---

## Tier 1 — Do today (site won’t work without these)

### Supabase
- [ ] Open [Supabase Dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard) → your project (`rrnucumzptbwdxtkccyx`)
- [ ] **SQL Editor** → run `supabase/00_reset.sql` (only if you haven’t already / need a clean slate)
- [ ] **SQL Editor** → run `supabase/schema.sql`
- [ ] **Settings → API** → copy:
  - Project URL → `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`
  - `anon` `public` key → `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`
  - `service_role` key → `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (never expose to client)

### Vercel env vars
**Project → Settings → Environment Variables** (Production + Preview):

| Variable | Value |
|----------|--------|
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` | From Supabase |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` | From Supabase |
| `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` | From Supabase (server only) |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` | `https://hyperank.ca` |
| `CRON_SECRET` | Random string (e.g. `openssl rand -hex 32`) |
| `ADMIN_USER_ID` | Your Supabase user UUID (see below) |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_RPM_SUBDOMAIN` | `hyperank` (after RPM setup) |
| `CONTACT_EMAIL` | `legal@hyperank.ca` |

- [ ] Redeploy after adding env vars (Deployments → ⋯ → Redeploy)

### Get your `ADMIN_USER_ID`
- [ ] Sign up on the site once (or create user in Supabase Auth)
- [ ] Supabase → **Authentication → Users** → copy your user **UUID**
- [ ] Paste into Vercel as `ADMIN_USER_ID` → redeploy

### GitHub → Vercel
- [ ] Push repo to GitHub (if not already)
- [ ] Vercel → **Import** repo → framework: Next.js → deploy
- [ ] Confirm build passes (it should — we verified locally)

### Seed trends + dictionary (empty site until you do this)
After deploy + env vars are set:

```bash
curl -X POST https://hyperank.ca/api/seed/trends \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_CRON_SECRET"

curl -X POST https://hyperank.ca/api/seed/dictionary \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_CRON_SECRET"
```

- [ ] Run both commands
- [ ] Homepage should show trends

---

## Tier 2 — This week (auth + domain)

### Supabase Auth — OAuth
**Authentication → Providers** — enable and add credentials:

- [ ] **Google** — [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com) OAuth client
- [ ] **Discord** — [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications)
- [ ] **Twitter/X** — [X Developer Portal](https://developer.twitter.com)

**Authentication → URL configuration:**

| Setting | Value |
|---------|--------|
| Site URL | `https://hyperank.ca` |
| Redirect URLs | `https://hyperank.ca/auth/callback` |
| | `http://localhost:3000/auth/callback` (for local dev) |

- [ ] Save and test login at `/login`

### Cloudflare DNS → Vercel
- [ ] Cloudflare → DNS for `hyperank.ca`:
  - `@` → CNAME → `cname.vercel-dns.com` (or A records per Vercel docs)
  - `www` → CNAME → `cname.vercel-dns.com`
- [ ] Vercel → **Settings → Domains** → add `hyperank.ca` + `www.hyperank.ca`
- [ ] Wait for SSL (Vercel + Cloudflare)

**Cloudflare SSL:** use **Full (strict)** once Vercel cert is active.

**Proxy (orange cloud):** fine for most traffic; if OAuth or WebSockets act up, try DNS-only temporarily to debug.

### Email confirmation (optional but recommended)
Supabase → **Authentication → Providers → Email**:
- [ ] Decide: require email confirm or not (spec wanted confirmation on)
- [ ] **Authentication → Email templates** — customize if you want

### Resend (parental consent / transactional email)
- [ ] Sign up at [resend.com](https://resend.com)
- [ ] Verify domain `hyperank.ca` (DNS records in Cloudflare)
- [ ] Add `RESEND_API_KEY` to Vercel
- [ ] Wire sending in app later (minor consent flow is UI-only for now)

---

## Tier 3 — Core product working end-to-end

### Ready Player Me (avatars)
- [ ] [readyplayer.me/developers](https://readyplayer.me/developers) → create app
- [ ] Subdomain: `hyperank` → matches `NEXT_PUBLIC_RPM_SUBDOMAIN`
- [ ] Allowed parent URLs: `https://hyperank.ca`, `http://localhost:3000`
- [ ] Test `/locker` → create avatar → should save to profile

### Vercel Cron jobs
`vercel.json` is already in the repo. On **Hobby** plan crons work but with limits.

- [ ] Vercel → **Settings → Cron Jobs** — confirm jobs listed after deploy
- [ ] Crons need `CRON_SECRET`; Vercel sends `Authorization: Bearer <CRON_SECRET>` if you configure it — **verify** in Vercel cron docs for your plan

**Manual test (any cron):**
```bash
curl https://hyperank.ca/api/cron/daily-drop \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_CRON_SECRET"
```

- [ ] Daily drop cron returns `{ ok: true }`
- [ ] Check homepage “Daily Drop” section next day (or after manual trigger)

### Create first battle (admin)
- [ ] `ADMIN_USER_ID` set and redeployed
- [ ] Visit `https://hyperank.ca/admin/battles`
- [ ] Create a 72h battle between two trends
- [ ] Vote at `/battles/[id]`

### Smoke test flows
- [ ] Sign up → age gate → OAuth → pick username (`/onboarding`)
- [ ] Vote on a trend (credits + streak update)
- [ ] Post a hot take on trend detail
- [ ] Place a prediction (10+ credits)
- [ ] View public profile `/u/yourusername`
- [ ] Leaderboard shows you after voting

---

## Tier 4 — Shop, marketplace, monetization

### Weekly shop drop (manual for now)
No admin UI for drops yet — create in Supabase **Table Editor** or SQL:

- [ ] Insert row in `shop_drops` (name, `drop_date`, `ends_at`, `is_active`)
- [ ] Insert rows in `shop_items` linked to `cosmetics` catalog (already seeded in schema)
- [ ] Test `/shop/drop` → buy with credits

### Marketplace
- [ ] Listings work; **buy from marketplace** RPC/UI still thin — test listing at `/shop/market/list`
- [ ] Optional: add `buy_marketplace_listing` RPC in Supabase if you want P2P sales before we build the UI

### Google AdSense (you had this on the old site)
- [ ] [google.com/adsense](https://google.com/adsense) — apply with `hyperank.ca`
- [ ] Keep `public/ads.txt` deployed (already in repo)
- [ ] Replace `<AdSlot />` placeholders with real AdSense units when approved
- [ ] Update **Cookie banner** + `/legal/cookies` when ad cookies go live

### Google Analytics (optional)
Old site used `G-ZC93ESXDWX`. If you want it back:
- [ ] Load gtag **only after** cookie consent = “Accept All”
- [ ] Add script in layout or a small analytics component

---

## Tier 5 — Security & compliance (before real traffic)

### Supabase RLS
- [ ] Confirm RLS enabled on all tables (schema does this)
- [ ] Never commit `.env.local` or service role key to GitHub

### Cloudflare security
- [ ] **SSL/TLS** → Full (strict)
- [ ] **WAF** → basic rules (block obvious bots if on free/pro plan)
- [ ] **Rate limiting** (Cloudflare) optional extra layer on `/api/*`

### Upstash Redis (optional — better rate limits)
App uses in-memory rate limits (resets on cold starts). For production:
- [ ] [upstash.com](https://upstash.com) → create Redis
- [ ] Add `UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL` + `UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN` to Vercel
- [ ] We’d wire these in code later (not required to launch)

### Legal pages
- [ ] Read `/legal/*` — replace placeholder emails/law province if needed
- [ ] Footer links work (already wired)

### COPPA / minors
- [ ] Age gate blocks under-13 (implemented)
- [ ] Parental consent email flow needs Resend + backend (not fully automated yet)

---

## Tier 6 — Later / nice to have

### Content & ops
- [ ] Add more trends (admin or SQL)
- [ ] Dictionary terms beyond seed JSON
- [ ] Brand applications → manually set `is_brand = true` in Supabase
- [ ] Sponsored trends → set `is_sponsored` + sponsor fields on `trends`
- [ ] Admin drop builder UI (`/admin/drops` is stub)
- [ ] Social verify automation (currently: user posts code, you verify manually)
- [ ] Discord bot for daily drop (spec nice-to-have)
- [ ] PWA / manifest polish
- [ ] 3D cosmetic GLB files in `/public/cosmetics/` (Blender pipeline from spec)

### Dev workflow
- [ ] Local: `.env.local` copy of Vercel env vars
- [ ] `npm run dev` for local testing
- [ ] GitHub: protect `main`, PR previews on Vercel
- [ ] Cloudflare: purge cache after big deploys if assets look stale

### Monitoring
- [ ] Vercel → Analytics (optional)
- [ ] Supabase → Database health + disk usage (free tier limits)
- [ ] Set up uptime check (UptimeRobot, Better Stack, etc.) on `https://hyperank.ca`

---

## Quick reference — your stack

```
GitHub (push) → Vercel (build + host + crons)
                    ↓
              hyperank.ca ← Cloudflare DNS/SSL
                    ↓
              Supabase (auth + DB)
              Ready Player Me (avatars)
              Resend (email, later)
```

---

## If something breaks — check in this order

1. Vercel env vars set and redeployed?
2. `00_reset.sql` + `schema.sql` run successfully?
3. Seed endpoints called with correct `CRON_SECRET`?
4. Supabase redirect URLs include `https://hyperank.ca/auth/callback`?
5. Cloudflare SSL mode = Full (strict)?
6. Browser console / Vercel function logs for API errors?

---

**Minimum path to “it works”:** Tier 1 entirely → Tier 2 OAuth + DNS → seed data → sign in and vote. That’s roughly **1–2 hours** if Supabase and Vercel are already connected.

_**Agent (default)**_

Fixed for **Vercel Hobby** limits.

## What changed

**`vercel.json`** — down from 12 crons to **2** (Hobby max, once per day each):

| When | Endpoint |
|------|----------|
| Midnight UTC daily | `/api/cron/daily` |
| Noon UTC Mondays | `/api/cron/weekly` |

**`/api/cron/daily`** runs everything that used to be spread across multiple jobs:
- Daily drop, vote velocity (24h window), predictions, battles, streaks, cosmetics, shop drop-end, trade expiry, floor prices, unbans
- Weekly report on **Sundays** only

**`/api/cron/weekly`** activates shop drops on **Mondays** only.

Individual routes like `/api/cron/daily-drop` still work if you trigger them manually with `CRON_SECRET` (useful for testing or external cron services).

## What you do

1. **Push** and redeploy on Vercel
2. Confirm **Settings → Cron Jobs** shows exactly **2** jobs
3. Test:
   ```bash
   curl https://hyperank.ca/api/cron/daily \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_CRON_SECRET"
   ```

`SETUP.md` is updated with Hobby cron notes.

**Tradeoff on Hobby:** vote velocity and battle resolution run **once per day**, not every 10/30 minutes. For that without Pro, use [cron-job.org](https://cron-job.org) or Cloudflare Workers to hit the individual `/api/cron/*` endpoints with your secret.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A massive design overhaul is needed. Every page, font, and icon will be customized. The red and black aesthetic will be refined into something clean, functional, and visually striking.

The design system will center on a deep red and near-black palette with mesh gradients, noise textures, and strong red glows. New utility classes and a custom scrollbar will be added. Shared components including an ambient background, page shell, section headers, and redesigned navigation will unify the interface.

Core UI primitives like Button, Card, Badge, and Input will be rebuilt. The login flow will drop the age gate entirely. Users will authenticate directly via OAuth, then complete a single birth year dropdown during onboarding if needed. The main layout will feature the ambient background and a bold custom font pairing. The homepage will become an epic hero with a stats ticker feel. Trend cards will be redesigned as much more visual elements. The login page will use a split layout with strong brand presence.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A comprehensive design system is being implemented across all key files.

A full redesign is planned with red-black aesthetics, ambient background effects, and custom UI components. The login flow will drop the age gate for a split-screen design. Age verification will be handled through metadata parsing and conditional redirects during onboarding. A new onboarding route will combine username entry with optional age verification.

The onboarding flow is being streamlined into a single `/onboarding` route. New users will enter a username and select a birth year from a dropdown. Existing users missing an age verification status will be redirected to a separate age-only screen.

A lightweight modal will handle age verification for logged-in users who lack it. The age selector will use a single birth-year dropdown with automatic blocking for underage users and minor-flagging for older entries.

The header will feature a glowing logo. The home page will showcase an animated hero with gradient text. Trend cards and heat bars will receive dramatic visual upgrades. The global layout will pair DM Sans, Syne, and JetBrains Mono fonts with an ambient background and page transition wrappers. The trends page will adopt the new shell component.

The dashboard, cookie banner, daily countdown, and credit display are being redesigned. A custom Logo component will be created using a hyperank icon paired with styled text. Lucide icons will be customized with red glow styling. Age helpers will parse birth years from user metadata to determine minor status and block underage access.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Implementing a full red/black visual overhaul and moving age verification to post-login.

_**Agent (default)**_

Done — two big changes are in place:

## Age gate (less annoying)

**Before:** Birth year blocked you on `/login` before OAuth.

**Now:**
1. `/login` — Google, Discord, or X immediately. No age wall.
2. New users → `/onboarding` — username + one birth-year dropdown (auto-filled from OAuth metadata when available).
3. Existing users missing `age_verified` → `/onboarding/age` — quick one-time screen.
4. Auth callback routes correctly based on profile state.

Minors still get `is_minor` / private profiles for compliance, but it happens after sign-in.

## Visual redesign (red/black, full send)

- **Ambient background** — mesh grid, red glow orbs, film grain on every page
- **Typography** — Syne (display), DM Sans (body), JetBrains Mono (stats/labels)
- **Homepage** — full hero with floating logo, live badge, gradient headlines, stat row
- **Trend cards** — glow borders, big hype %, animated heat bar, confetti on bonus credits
- **Header/Footer/Mobile nav** — glass blur, active pill states, custom SVG icons
- **Pages restyled** — trends, battles, leaderboard, dashboard, locker, shop drop, profiles, trend detail
- **UI primitives** — buttons, cards, badges, inputs all match the red/black vibe

Build passes clean (`npm run build` ✓).

Run `npm run dev` and hit `/login` and `/` to see it. Admin, legal, and settings pages still use the older shell — say if you want those pushed through the same treatment next.

---

_**User**_

yes but more profesional, while still being consitnrnt w/ theme

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Refining the design to be more professional while keeping it consistent with the theme.

Toning down the aggressive visual effects. Cards lose their hover transforms and mouse-tracking gradients. Buttons and labels get softer shadows and tighter tracking. Background glow orbs and noise overlays are reduced. Emojis are removed from the homepage stats.

The hero headline shrinks to text-5xl and the floating logo animation disappears. Placeholder stats are replaced with real data or removed entirely. Trend card buttons drop emojis and aggressive hover motion. The corner glow blob is removed. Hype percentages shrink. Confetti stays subtle. Buttons shift to rounded-lg corners instead of full pills.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A comprehensive professional refinement is planned.

The plan targets globals.css specifically. Cards will use solid rounded backgrounds with reduced glass effects. Hover states will shift borders subtly without translation. Buttons will adopt solid colors and minimal shadows. Labels will use tighter tracking and muted red. Live indicators will lose their heavy box-shadow. Tailwind will receive a refined hype color and professional shadow.

Buttons will switch to rounded-lg with solid red backgrounds and no default gradient. Badges will be more restrained. Ambient glows will fade to 0.08–0.12 opacity. The logo will drop its blur glow for a single red accent. The header will use a 56px height with a bottom border indicator and a standard button. The homepage hero will lose fake statistics and floating elements. Trend cards will keep only essential text and minimal motion.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Refined the UI toward a more professional product feel while keeping the red/black HypeRank identity.

**What changed**

- **Restrained palette** — deeper charcoal backgrounds (`#090909` / `#111`), muted red accent (`#e82222`), zinc text hierarchy instead of heavy glows
- **Cleaner surfaces** — solid cards with subtle borders, no floating animations, mouse-tracking effects, or aggressive hover lifts
- **Typography** — semibold headlines, professional copy (“Culture intelligence”, “Measure what’s hype”), no emoji in UI
- **Buttons & inputs** — `rounded-lg`, solid fills, minimal shadows (more SaaS, less arcade)
- **Navigation** — underline active states, compact header, text-only mobile nav
- **Trend cards** — clean Hype/Dead labels, tabular numbers, simple progress bar
- **Login & onboarding** — split-panel auth, formal legal copy, compliance-focused age screen
- **Dashboard & footer** — structured stats cards, descriptive quick links, product/legal columns

The theme is still unmistakably HypeRank — red accents, dark mode, Syne + DM Sans — but it reads more like a real platform than a demo. Build passes clean.

If you want to push further, admin/legal/settings pages are the next batch to align with this system.

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Three separate issues — all addressed:

## 1. `406 Not Acceptable` on profiles

That happens when you're **signed in but have no profile row yet** (onboarding not finished). The app was calling `.single()`, which Supabase treats as an error when zero rows come back.

**Fixed** in `UserProvider`, `CreditDisplay`, onboarding, and auth callback — now using `.maybeSingle()` so missing profiles don't throw.

**What you should do:** visit `/onboarding` and finish username + birth year. Until then, credits/header profile won't show — that's expected.

---

## 2. Seed curls showing `Redirecting...`

`hyperank.ca` **308-redirects to `www.hyperank.ca`**. Plain `curl` doesn't follow that, so the POST never hits the API.

Use **`www`** or **`-L`**:

```bash
export CRON_SECRET="your-vercel-cron-secret"

curl -L -X POST https://www.hyperank.ca/api/seed/trends \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CRON_SECRET"

curl -L -X POST https://www.hyperank.ca/api/seed/dictionary \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CRON_SECRET"
```

You should get JSON back, e.g. `{"ok":true,"seeded":42}` — not `Redirecting...`.

**Also:** set `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` in Vercel to `https://www.hyperank.ca` (not bare `hyperank.ca`).

---

## 3. Local `.env` template

Created **`.env.local.example`** — copy and fill in:

```bash
cp .env.local.example .env.local
```

Then add your Supabase keys and `CRON_SECRET`. Local seeding:

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/seed/trends \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CRON_SECRET"
```

`SETUP.md` is updated with these commands (and the exposed secret from the doc was removed — rotate `CRON_SECRET` in Vercel if that value was committed anywhere public).

---

_**User**_

@/Users/noahfallu/.cursor/projects/Users-noahfallu-Documents-GitHub-Personal-hyperank/terminals/6.txt:7-42 @/Users/noahfallu/.cursor/projects/Users-noahfallu-Documents-GitHub-Personal-hyperank/terminals/7.txt:7-20 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The local seed API is returning a 401 Unauthorized error.

The curl command uses a production CRON_SECRET, but `.env.local` contains a placeholder secret. This mismatch causes the 401 error.

Either replace the placeholder in `.env.local` with the production secret, or use the local secret in the curl command.

Adding a comment to `.env.local.example` could prevent future mismatches. A clearer dev-mode error message would also help. The 401 response confirms the placeholder secret is still in use.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Production seed worked. Local 401 is a CRON_SECRET mismatch: `.env.local` still has the placeholder while curl uses your production secret. Checking and clarifying the env template.

_**Agent (default)**_

Production seeding worked — 25 trends and 25 dictionary entries are in.

The local **401** is a secret mismatch, not a broken API:

| Where | `CRON_SECRET` value |
|--------|---------------------|
| Your curl (`export CRON_SECRET=4f86…`) | Production Vercel secret |
| `.env.local` (from the example file) | `local-dev-secret-change-me` |

The dev server checks against `.env.local`, so they have to match.

**Fix — pick one:**

**Option A** — Use production secret locally (edit `.env.local`):
```bash
CRON_SECRET=4f8637fa1e533ad8ecf2583ab8e84b1e70b6fc0945b86b58cc3b10232b1e5d29
```
Restart `npm run dev`, then run your curl again.

**Option B** — Keep the placeholder in `.env.local` and curl with that:
```bash
export CRON_SECRET="local-dev-secret-change-me"
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/seed/trends \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CRON_SECRET"
```

Restart the dev server after any `.env.local` change — Next only loads env vars at startup.

You don’t need to re-seed locally if production already succeeded; both hit the same Supabase project. Local seed is only useful when testing against a different DB or before deploy.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The auth callback is failing because the route handler creates a client using `createClient()` from `server.ts`, which relies on `next/headers` cookies. In Route Handlers, `exchangeCodeForSession` must attach cookies directly to the redirect response rather than through the cookie store alone. This explains why the sign-in flow produces no console logs and leaves the UI stuck on "sign in".

The standard Supabase Next.js App Router callback pattern uses `createServerClient` with `next/headers` cookies. It exchanges the authorization code for a session and attaches the resulting cookies directly to the `NextResponse` redirect.

The callback handler validates the `next` parameter and redirects to the local origin in development or to the forwarded host in production.

The real bug is that Next.js 14 route handlers cannot use `cookies()` from `next/headers` inside route handlers. The correct approach is to use `createServerClient` from `@supabase/ssr` instead, which writes cookies directly onto the `NextResponse` redirect.

The callback route now imports `createServerClient` from `@supabase/ssr` and passes a custom cookie store. This store implements `getAll` and `setAll` methods to read and write cookies directly onto the `NextResponse` redirect.

The `setAll` method iterates over each cookie to apply it directly to the response. The middleware currently uses the older `get/set/remove` cookie API, which may conflict with the newer `getAll/setAll` pattern.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The auth callback likely fails because Next.js App Router route handlers using `cookies()` from `next/headers` with the server `createClient` do not properly set session cookies during redirects.

The fix uses `createServerClient` from `@supabase/ssr` 0.12 within the Next.js 14 route handler. It extracts the authorization code and validates the `next` redirect parameter before processing the callback.

It configures `createServerClient` with custom cookie getters and setters that wrap `next/headers`. Then it exchanges the authorization code for a session.

The callback handler now redirects to the validated `next` path or an error page if the code exchange fails. A potential version mismatch was noted: `server.ts` awaits `cookies()`, which Next.js 14.2.35 treats as synchronous, so the async pattern may not match the installed runtime.

Awaiting `cookies()` is safe even though Next.js 14 treats it as synchronous.

The real issue is that `NextResponse.redirect()` may drop cookies set during `exchangeCodeForSession`.

The fix is to build the response object first, then attach the session cookies to it before returning the redirect.

The cookie setter now attaches session cookies directly to the `NextResponse.redirect` object before returning it. The official Supabase Next.js template is being checked for the exact callback implementation.

The callback handler now creates a `NextResponse.next` object and attaches session cookies to it before returning. After successfully exchanging the authorization code, it checks the forwarded host header and development environment to determine the correct redirect origin.

The callback handler now redirects to the local origin in development, or to the forwarded host in production. The `supabaseResponse` variable pattern used earlier is too tricky. A cleaner implementation is being drafted.

The callback handler now reads the `redirect` parameter instead of `next`, defaulting to `/dashboard`. It awaits `cookies()` synchronously and passes those cookies into `createServerClient` with custom `getAll` and `setAll` methods. The `setAll` implementation begins iterating over the cookies to apply them.

The callback handler now applies cookies through `cookieStore.set` inside `setAll`, with a fallback for Server Components. After successfully exchanging the authorization code, it redirects to the validated path; otherwise it returns to `/login?error=auth`. `cookieStore.set` is confirmed to work correctly in Next.js 14 route handlers.

A dedicated `lib/supabase/route-handler.ts` file will house the callback logic. Middleware will be updated to use `getAll` and `setAll` for cookie compatibility.

The callback route will be rewritten with proper cookie handling and will use `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` for OAuth redirects.

The Header will display a signed-in state whenever a user exists, linking to `/onboarding` or `/dashboard` even without a profile. `onAuthStateChange` will be added to `HeaderClient` and `UserProvider`.

The login page will gain tabbed views for OAuth, email sign-in, email sign-up, and forgot-password flows.

A new `/auth/update-password` page will handle setting a new password after email recovery links. The password reset flow will call `resetPasswordForEmail` with a redirect back to the callback route. Email sign-up will use `emailRedirectTo` to land users on the callback after they confirm their address. The OAuth redirect URLs will use `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` to prevent `www` subdomain mismatches.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Fixed several auth issues and added email login. Here's what was going wrong and what changed:

## Why sign-in looked broken

1. **Session cookies weren't sticking** — the OAuth callback wasn't writing cookies to the redirect response correctly, so you'd come back from Google/Discord with no session.
2. **Header required a full profile** — it only showed you as logged in if `user && profile`. OAuth users without onboarding still saw **Sign in**.
3. **No auth listener** — the header only checked once on load, so it never updated after login.

## What's fixed

- **Auth callback** — proper cookie handling on redirect (session persists)
- **OAuth redirect** — uses `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` (must be `https://www.hyperank.ca`)
- **Header** — shows **Finish setup** when signed in but no profile; username when profile exists
- **`onAuthStateChange`** — session updates across the app in real time
- **Email/password** — sign in, sign up, forgot password on `/login`
- **Password reset** — `/auth/update-password` after email link

## What you need in Supabase

1. **URL config** (Authentication → URL configuration):
   - Site URL: `https://www.hyperank.ca`
   - Redirect URLs: `https://www.hyperank.ca/auth/callback` and `http://localhost:3000/auth/callback`

2. **Email provider** — enable under Authentication → Providers → Email

3. **Vercel** — `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL=https://www.hyperank.ca` (deploy after changing)

## After deploy

1. Sign in with Google/Discord → you should land on **/onboarding** (first time) or **/dashboard**
2. Header should show **Finish setup** or your username — not **Sign in**
3. Email flow: sign up → confirm email → sign in, or use **Forgot password?**

If OAuth still fails after deploy, check Supabase Auth logs — a redirect URL mismatch is the usual cause.

---

Admin features still need to be connected and made functional. The 3D character embed fails because the CSP `frame-src` allows `readyplayer.me` but the site uses `hyperank.readyplayer.me`. Default cosmetics need seed data in the shop. All remaining master prompt features still need to be fully implemented.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here's what was going wrong and what I fixed:

## Voting 403

Two causes:

1. **Not signed in** → API returns `401` (now opens login modal)
2. **Signed in but no profile** → API returns `403` because onboarding wasn't finished (username + birth year)

The header used to show "Sign in" even when you were logged in but hadn't onboarded. It now shows **Finish setup** → `/onboarding`.

**To vote:** sign in → complete onboarding → vote works.

## New: login modal

Unsigned users get a fade-in modal (Google, Discord, email) when they try to vote, predict, battle vote, or shop buy.

## RPM / character editor CSP

`hyperank.readyplayer.me` was blocked because CSP only allowed `readyplayer.me`. Updated to allow `https://*.readyplayer.me`. **Redeploy** for this to take effect on production.

## Shop & cosmetics

- Cosmetics are already in `schema.sql` (beanie, tees, crowns, etc.)
- New **`POST /api/seed/shop`** creates an active weekly drop with purchasable items
- **Buy now** actually calls `/api/shop/purchase` (was a dead link before)
- Onboarding grants starter cosmetics: beanie, white tee, HypeRank tee, sneakers

```bash
curl -L -X POST https://www.hyperank.ca/api/seed/shop \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CRON_SECRET"
```

## Admin (wired up)

- **Drops** — create active shop drops from cosmetics catalog
- **Reports** — resolve/dismiss buttons
- **Battles** — already worked via form
- Back links on admin, locker, battles

## SETUP.md

Added **"Your action items"** at the top — things only you can do (RPM subdomain, admin UUID, seeds, etc.).

---

## Master prompt — honest status

| Area | Status |
|------|--------|
| Trends, voting, streaks, battles | Works after sign-in + onboarding |
| Auth (OAuth + email + forgot password) | Done — deploy + Supabase email config |
| 3D avatar (RPM iframe) | Works after CSP deploy + RPM subdomain setup |
| Cosmetics in DB | In schema; shop needs seed |
| Marketplace buy flow | Partial — list exists; full buy RPC may need testing |
| Admin panel | Core actions work; some pages still light |
| Realtime, ads, trade accept UI | Not fully wired |
| GLB cosmetic files in `/public` | Not uploaded (RPM handles avatar mesh) |

**Your immediate checklist:**
1. Deploy this build
2. Sign in → finish **onboarding**
3. Set `ADMIN_USER_ID` to your UUID
4. Run seed shop curl
5. Configure RPM subdomain + parent URL in Ready Player Me dashboard

If you want the full master prompt completed end-to-end, send it again and I'll work through the remaining gaps (marketplace buy, trades UI, sponsored trends admin, realtime, etc.) in priority order.

---

_**User**_

visit: https://github.com/readyplayerme/visage : Visage GitHub license npm version
Visage is a collection of components for showcasing Ready Player Me avatars and 3D on the web!

Built with three.js, react-three-fiber, drei, three-stdlib and react.

Installation
Visage is available as an npm package.

npm install @readyplayerme/visage
Documentation & examples
You can find all code examples of the components and their documentation on our GitHub page.

Here is the first one to get you started:

import React from 'react';
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom';
import { Avatar } from '@readyplayerme/visage';

const modelSrc = 'https://readyplayerme.github.io/visage/male.glb';

function App() {
  return (
    <Avatar modelSrc={modelSrc} />
  );
}

ReactDOM.render(<App />, document.querySelector('#app'));
Validation
Props such as modelSrc, animationSrc, poseSrc and onLoadedAnimation.src are validated before rendering on the scene. Animation source props support both .glb and .fbx formats. All other props mentioned before only support .glb.

Examples on supported resource formats:

URL resources
relative /headwear.glb
absolute https://readyplayerme.github.io/visage/male.glb?queryParams=allowed
Base64 strings
data:application/octet-stream;base64
data:model/gltf-binary;base64
Binary input such as model/gltf-binary

More details can be found at web integration guides.

Ready Player Me - example-iframe

About Ready Player Me
Ready Player Me is a cross-game avatar platform for Unity, Unreal Engine, and all web-based stacks.

Getting Started
Read the documentation to get started with integrating Ready Player Me or explore the examples.

Add Ready Player Me avatars to your app or game
Want to add Ready Player Me avatars to your own app or game? Apply here.

... and - https://github.com/readyplayerme/rpm-react-avatar-creator : Ready Player Me - React Avatar Creator
Ready Player Me - React Avatar Creator is a set of components and helper methods to help implementing the Ready Player Me Avatar Creator into React projects.

Codesandbox example for loading the Avatar Creator: https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/ready-player-me-react-avatar-creator-qxkqjf

Codesandbox example for loading the Avatar Creator and visualizing the avatar with the Visage package: https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/ready-player-me-visage-example-l4m2k2

Installation
Ready Player Me React Avatar Creator is available as a npm package.

npm i @readyplayerme/react-avatar-creator
Usage
import { AvatarCreator } from '@readyplayerme/react-avatar-creator';

export default function App() {
  return <AvatarCreator subdomain="demo" style={{ width: '100%', height: '100vh', border: 'none' }} />;
}
 how-it-works.mp4 
Components
AvatarCreator
AvatarCreator component helps you load Ready Player Me in an iframe where you can edit your avatar and receive your avatar URL as a post message once your avatar is exported.

Parameters
subdomain [required]: string

Your Ready Player Me subdomain. You can get one from Ready Player Me Studio.
className [optional]: string

The css classes to apply to the iframe.
style [optional]: CSSProperties

The css styles to apply to the iframe.
config [optional]: AvatarCreatorConfig

Editor Configuration is where you can set url properties of Ready Player Me editor. Read more about these options in Ready Player Me documentations.
onAvatarExported [optional]: (event: AvatarExportedEvent) => void

Callback function that is called when avatar is exported.
onUserSet [optional]: (event: UserSetEvent) => void

Callback function that is called when user id is set.
onAssetUnlocked [optional]: (event: AssetUnlockedEvent) => void

Callback function that is called when an asset is unlocked.
onUserAuthorized [optional]: (event: UserAuthorizedEvent) => void

Callback function that is called when the user is authorized.
Example
import { AvatarCreator, AvatarCreatorConfig, AvatarExportedEvent, UserSetEvent } from '@readyplayerme/react-avatar-creator';

const config: AvatarCreatorConfig = {
  clearCache: true,
  bodyType: 'fullbody',
  quickStart: false,
  language: 'en',
};

const style = { width: '100%', height: '100vh', border: 'none' };

export default function App() {
  const handleOnUserSet = (event: UserSetEvent) => {
    console.log(`User ID is: ${event.data.id}`);
  };

  const handleOnAvatarExported = (event: AvatarExportedEvent) => {
    console.log(`Avatar URL is: ${event.data.url}`);
  };

  return (
    <>
      <AvatarCreator subdomain="demo" config={config} style={style} onUserSet={handleOnUserSet} onAvatarExported={handleOnAvatarExported} />
    </>
  );
}
AvatarCreatorRaw
AvatarCreatorRaw is a lower level component that gives you everything found in the avatar creator, but without explicit callbacks for each event, so you can have the ability to create your own custom logic around these events, if you choose to do so.

Parameters
subdomain [required]: string

Your Ready Player Me subdomain. You can get one from Ready Player Me Studio.
className [optional]: string

The css classes to apply to the iframe.
style [optional]: CSSProperties

The css styles to apply to the iframe.
config [optional]: AvatarCreatorConfig

Editor Configuration is where you can set url properties of Ready Player Me editor. Read more about these options in Ready Player Me documentations.
onEventReceived [required]: (event: IFrameEvent) => void

Callback function that is called whenever an AvatarCreatorEvent is published
Example
import { AvatarCreatorConfig, AvatarCreatorEvent, AvatarCreatorRaw } from '@readyplayerme/react-avatar-creator';

const config: AvatarCreatorConfig = {
  clearCache: true,
  bodyType: 'fullbody',
  quickStart: false,
  language: 'en',
};

const style = { width: '100%', height: '100vh', border: 'none' };

export default function App() {
  const handleCustomEvent = (event: AvatarCreatorEvent) => {
    console.log(`Received custom event`, event);
  };

  return (
    <>
      <AvatarCreatorRaw subdomain="demo" config={config} style={style} onEventReceived={handleCustomEvent} />
    </>
  );
}
Using AvatarCreator with Visage
If you would like to use Visage, with its full capability to edit camera and light properties of the scene and more, you can use AvatarCreator component and Visage components together.

import { Avatar } from '@readyplayerme/visage';
import { AvatarCreator, AvatarCreatorConfig } from '@readyplayerme/react-avatar-creator';
import { useState } from 'react';

const subdomain = 'demo';

const config: AvatarCreatorConfig = {
  clearCache: true,
  bodyType: 'fullbody',
  quickStart: false,
  language: 'en',
};

const style = { width: '100%', height: '100vh', border: 'none' };

export const YourCustomComponent = () => {
  const [url, setUrl] = useState<string>();

  if (!url) {
    return <AvatarCreator subdomain={subdomain} config={config} style={style} onAvatarExported={(event) => setUrl(event.data.url)} />;
  }
  return <Avatar style={style} modelSrc={url} cameraInitialDistance={10} />;
};... THOUGH DOUBLE CHEKC TO CONFIRM BUT APPARENTLY: https://techcrunch.com/2025/12/19/netflix-acquires-gaming-avatar-maker-ready-player-me/  long story short, netflixh bought it, and killed the service, what do we do now?

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Ready Player Me was acquired by Netflix and shut down its service. A replacement solution is needed.

A self-hosted avatar builder using React Three Fiber is being considered. Options include a default GLB model, a JSON configuration for customizations, or a simple procedural 3D mesh.

The pragmatic plan replaces the RPM editor with an `AvatarBuilder` component offering color pickers and preset body types. Self-hosted avatar data will live in `avatar_model_url` and `avatar_config`, while the legacy `avatar_rpm_url` column remains for backward compatibility. A default GLB model can be sourced from CC0 libraries or GitHub raw content.

A procedural avatar built from Three.js primitives is the most reliable path. It avoids GLB files and external dependencies entirely. Colors from `avatar_config` will drive the body, hair, and clothing. Locker cosmetics can override or add geometry to the base figure.

The Avatar3D component will fall back to a procedural figure when no valid GLB model is present. AvatarBuilder will offer skin tone, hair color, and body preset controls, storing selections in avatar_config. The implementation will split into three parts: a shared AvatarConfig type, a procedural AvatarFigure component, and the AvatarBuilder UI.

The implementation will touch Avatar3D, the locker, settings, and documentation. A sentinel model URL constant will mark self-hosted procedural avatars. Dead RPM links will fall back to the procedural figure. Setup instructions and Next.js CSP entries will be stripped of all RPM references.

The Avatar3D fallback logic will render the procedural figure when the model URL is missing or points to the dead Ready Player Me domain. A PATCH endpoint will accept `avatar_config` and set `avatar_rpm_url` to the `hyperank:procedural` sentinel. A PUT route on the avatar endpoint will handle combined saves.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The avatar configuration structure needs fixing.

CosmeticsLocker stores equipped cosmetics under `avatar_config`, but AvatarBuilder was overwriting them by saving appearance data there too. The fix is to separate the two: keep equipped cosmetics at the top level and nest appearance settings under a new `appearance` key.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Unifying the avatar configuration structure.

Defining a unified `AvatarConfigRoot` interface with nested `equipped` and `appearance` fields. Legacy top-level keys will be parsed into the correct structure. CosmeticsLocker will save equipped items, while AvatarBuilder will store appearance settings and the avatar URL.

Simpler approach: keep equipped cosmetics at the top level and nest builder colors under `appearance`. CosmeticsLocker must merge rather than overwrite appearance data. AvatarBuilder PUT will merge the incoming appearance object into the existing avatar config.

The API routes are now defined. The PUT handler merges the incoming appearance object into the existing avatar config. The POST handler spreads equipped items at the top level to preserve backward compatibility with ProfileHero. Background keys map directly to the existing `avatar_config.background` field.

Adding a `getEquippedFromConfig` helper to extract equipped cosmetics from top-level avatar config keys. This reads hat, shirt, pants, shoes, accessory, background, and effect directly from the root object instead of a nested structure.

A `getAppearanceFromConfig` helper is being added to extract nested appearance values and merge them with default avatar settings. AvatarBuilder will only send appearance data in its PUT requests. CosmeticsLocker will merge saved equipped items with appearance data before passing both sets to Avatar3D.

The locker page will show the builder for users without a custom GLB avatar. A missing RPM URL or procedural avatar flag triggers the builder view. On save, the system stores a procedural avatar URL. Onboarding may pre-set a default avatar on redirect to the locker.

The avatar display logic is simplified. A missing RPM URL will trigger the builder first. After saving, the system stores a procedural avatar URL and shows the locker. Existing users with dead RPM URLs will see the builder optionally in the edit section.

Legacy RPM URLs will render procedurally with existing cosmetics. Users can rebuild in the editor section.

CosmeticsLocker will accept an avatarConfig prop and pass it to Avatar3D along with derived equipped items. The API POST handler will merge incoming equipped items into the existing avatar_config record.

ProfileHero will pass the full avatar_config to Avatar3D. Leaderboard and battle views will also be updated to use the new configuration structure.

Leaderboard queries will now select avatar_config to support equipped cosmetics. The RPMEditor component will be deleted and all references removed. Setup documentation and environment variables will drop the Ready Player Me subdomain. Next.js content security policy will exclude readyplayer.me. The avatar API PUT handler and database types for avatar_config will be fixed.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Unifying `avatar_config` so appearance and equipped cosmetics don't overwrite each other.


_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The build passed. I will explain what happened to Ready Player Me and how to proceed.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

You're right — **Ready Player Me is dead for third-party integrations.**

Netflix acquired RPM in December 2025 and wound down the public platform on **January 31, 2026** ([TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2025/12/19/netflix-acquires-gaming-avatar-maker-ready-player-me/)). That means:

| What you found | Status |
|---|---|
| [visage](https://github.com/readyplayerme/visage) | Display-only — needs a GLB URL; RPM CDN is gone |
| [Example-iframe](https://github.com/readyplayerme/Example-iframe) | Dead — iframe creator no longer exists |
| [@readyplayerme/react-avatar-creator](https://github.com/readyplayerme/rpm-react-avatar-creator) | Dead — same iframe dependency |

**Do not build on RPM anymore.** Netflix is using the tech internally for Netflix Games, not as a public SDK.

---

## What we did instead

HypeRank now uses a **built-in procedural 3D avatar** (React Three Fiber, no external service):

1. **`AvatarBuilder`** — skin, hair, shirt colors, body type at `/locker` and `/settings/avatar`
2. **`AvatarFigure`** — low-poly blocky character that renders in the locker, profile, leaderboard, etc.
3. **Cosmetics still work** — hats, shirts, effects map to visual overrides on the procedural model
4. **Legacy RPM URLs** — users with old `readyplayer.me` links automatically fall back to the built-in avatar
5. **Removed** — `RPMEditor`, CSP `frame-src` for RPM, `NEXT_PUBLIC_RPM_SUBDOMAIN` from `.env.example` and `SETUP.md`

Flow: sign in → `/locker` → customize → **Save character** → equip cosmetics in the locker above.

---

## What you need to do

1. **Redeploy** to production (CSP + new avatar code)
2. **Remove** `NEXT_PUBLIC_RPM_SUBDOMAIN` from Vercel env vars if it's still there
3. **Test** `/locker` — build a character and save an outfit

---

## If you want fancier avatars later

| Option | Effort | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Keep procedural + more cosmetics** | Low | Easiest path; fits the game vibe |
| **Self-hosted GLB base models** | Medium | Host CC0 models in `/public`, keep R3F loader |
| **VRoid / custom pipeline** | High | Users export GLB elsewhere, you store URLs |
| **2D layered avatars** | Low | No WebGL, very reliable on mobile |

The `avatar_rpm_url` column name is kept for DB compatibility — it now stores `hyperank:procedural` or a self-hosted GLB URL if you add that later.

Build passes locally. After redeploy, avatar creation should work without any external account.

---

The first step is fixing the page refresh issue in the CosmeticsLocker.

Removing `window.location.reload()` and adding `type="button"` to buttons will stop the refresh. A ConfirmDialog component will handle purchase and save confirmations. AvatarConfig will expand to include gender, face types, hair styles, eye color, and optional lip color.

Jewelry cosmetics will be added to the database and schema. Watch, chain, and earrings will each get their own equip slots. These items will be categorized under jewelry or accessories.

A preview-only try-on system will let users test unowned cosmetics without saving. Unowned items will display a "Preview" badge and a "Buy" button showing credits. The save function will only commit owned items, stripping any previewed unowned pieces before saving.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Creating a reusable `ConfirmDialog.tsx` component for confirmation modals.

Expanding avatar types and builder with gender, faces, hair, and jewelry. Rewriting the cosmetics locker for try-on without page refreshes. Adding jewelry slots and owned item tracking. Updating onboarding to a multi-step avatar selection.

Purchase confirmations will display item names, costs, and credit balances. Outfit saves will be blocked if any equipped cosmetic is unowned. A helper function will detect unowned items in the current preview.

Unowned items will appear as "Try-on" previews. The bottom action bar will switch to a "Buy for X credits" button for unowned selections. Outfit saves will be restricted to owned items only, with a warning if any preview slot contains a locked cosmetic.

The save button will be disabled whenever the preview includes any unowned item, or when the display matches the saved state exactly. Cosmetics Locker will manage its own local owned IDs state, initialized from parent props. Gender-specific body shapes will be added to the AvatarFigure component.

Female avatars will have narrower shoulders and wider hips. Face types include square, round, sharp, and soft shapes. Hair options cover short, buzz, long, ponytail, and bald. Jewelry pieces will be watches, chains, and earrings. New jewelry entries will be added to the database schema.

Adding pants categories to the cosmetics schema. Onboarding will split into two steps: first username and birth year, then avatar customization. The avatar builder will run in a compact mode without a save button during onboarding. The profile will be created in the first step, and the avatar will be saved separately in the second step.

Finalizing the onboarding flow: step one collects username and birth year, while step two embeds the full avatar builder. A single "Finish setup" button at the end will save both the profile and the avatar configuration together.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here's what changed:

## Locker refresh fix
The page was reloading because clicking unowned items triggered an **instant purchase** followed by `window.location.reload()`. That's gone.

- All locker buttons are `type="button"` (no accidental form submits)
- Clicks **try on** items in local state only — no navigation, no reload
- Purchases update credits/owned items in memory after the API call

## Try-on + buy flow
- **Any item** → click to preview on your character
- **Owned** → equip in preview, then **Save outfit** (with confirmation)
- **Locked (costs credits)** → preview shows "Try-on" badge + **Buy [name] — X credits** button (with confirmation showing balance after)
- **Streak/badge unlocks** → preview only, with unlock requirement shown
- **Save outfit** is disabled while previewing unowned items

## More character customization
**Onboarding** is now 2 steps:
1. Username + birth year  
2. Full character builder → **Finish setup** (with confirmation)

**Avatar builder** now includes:
- **Gender** — male / female (different proportions)
- **Body** — default, tall, stocky
- **Face** — default, round, sharp, soft
- **Hair** — short, buzz, long, ponytail, bald
- **Colors** — skin, hair, eyes, shirt, pants

## Jewelry + pants (new shop categories)
- **Watch** — Silver Watch, Gold Watch  
- **Chain** — Silver Chain, Gold Chain  
- **Earrings** — Gold Studs, Silver Hoops, Ice Studs  
- **Pants** — Black Joggers (free), Blue Jeans, Camo Pants  

## Confirmations
New `ConfirmDialog` for:
- Save character (locker/settings)
- Save outfit (locker)
- Buy cosmetic (locker)
- Finish setup (onboarding)

---

**If your Supabase DB already exists**, run `supabase/patch_cosmetics_jewelry.sql` in the SQL Editor so the new jewelry/pants items appear. Then redeploy.

---

_**User**_

fix profile icons, first off i see when i vote on battles, etc i see my characer spinning, etc.. first off make it a image so no spinning, etc and also the character is way too high in the circle so bring him down and focus on more general face chest, etc still show most of whole body tho, BUT also the user profile icon needs to be a global thing, so they can set their profile icon in their profile but they can select like diferent poses for their character, etc and its kinda like a cool 3d scene where teir charcater is on a mini stage and theres a camera 2d block camera and has a take photo button and then it flashes and reveals their profile icon but they can like crop it, etc and then save.. make it interesting, in the photo it should legit take a photo of their character how it is like with their customizations, poses, and even lets make backgorunds so you can get cool backgrounds as another cosmetics, etc

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants profile icons converted from spinning 3D characters to static images. They want the avatar positioned lower in the circular frame to show more chest area while keeping the full body visible. They also want a global profile icon system with selectable poses, a 3D mini-stage camera interface with a photo capture feature, cropping options, and background cosmetics.

I am planning to store a static `avatar_url` on the profile schema and generate it from the photo studio. Small avatars in battles and leaderboards will render this static image instead of the spinning 3D canvas. I will also check the current profile page and battle avatar logic.</details></think>

Exploring how avatars and profile images are used across the app, then implementing a static profile-icon system with a photo studio.

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Planning a `ProfileAvatar` component. It will render a circular image from `avatar_url`. A gradient fallback with initials or a generic silhouette will appear if the image fails to load.

Designing an `AvatarStudio` photo studio with a fixed camera and no spinning. An R3F `AvatarStageScene` will render the character on a mini stage. A new `/api/avatar/photo` endpoint will upload base64 images to Supabase storage and save the pose and crop config. Need to check Supabase bucket policies.

Will add SQL to create the Supabase storage bucket. The studio will capture WebGL screenshots by enabling `preserveDrawingBuffer` on the R3F canvas. Procedural figure poses will be driven by props: default, wave, flex, peace, and lean. Backgrounds will come from equipped cosmetic items.

The studio layout will place the 3D scene with a viewfinder frame overlay on the left. The right panel will hold a pose picker, a background picker, and a Take Photo button. After capture, a flash modal will reveal the image for cropping via canvas drag and zoom. Saving will trigger a confirmation dialog. The `avatar_config` schema will store the selected pose, a normalized crop rectangle, and the active background cosmetic ID.

The pose will live inside the appearance object rather than the avatar config. A new `ProfileAvatarClient` component will accept an optional avatar URL, username, size, and className. Sizes will range from 32px to 120px. Queries for battles, leaderboards, and the shop market must be updated to fetch `avatar_url` instead of `avatar_rpm_url`.

A dedicated `/settings/profile-photo` page will host the AvatarStudio. The HeaderClient will render the new ProfileAvatar using the live `avatar_url`. Locker links will also point to the studio. Implementation will split into pose definitions, background configs, a pose-aware AvatarStageFigure, and the main AvatarStudio component.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
BattleCard isn't fetching voter data, so the character spin animation likely has no character to render.

Updating types for when voter data populates. Setting up Supabase storage for avatars with an admin client and authenticated upload policies.

Storage policies now restrict uploads and updates to each user's own folder while allowing public reads. The API route will use the user's Supabase client to upload avatars to a per-user path. Canvas-based cropping will handle the final image with a circular or square overlay and a zoom slider.

The AvatarStudio interface will let users drag to pan the scene. A white flash overlay will simulate a camera shutter before capturing the canvas. The AvatarFigure component will be refactored to accept an animate prop so it can render still images instead of spinning animations. The flow will load the user's profile data, pose and background grids, a viewfinder UI, a flash capture, cropping, and then a save step.

A new `AvatarStageScene` component will handle the studio camera and fixed rotation, keeping the locker version simpler. The `ProfileAvatar` component will render the saved avatar image with a centered crop, or fall back to a gradient placeholder showing the first letter of the username.

The studio camera will be lowered and aimed at chest level to center the face and torso in the frame. The character group will shift downward on the stage, and a platform mesh will sit beneath it. Backgrounds will be rendered as colored planes or textures tied to cosmetic selections. Pose changes will adjust arm mesh positions directly inside the stage figure. New fields `profile_pose` and `studio_background` will be added to the appearance configuration to store the selected pose and background.

The final cropped image will be uploaded directly, so no crop data needs to be stored in the database. The pose and background selections will be saved to a new `profile_photo` field inside the appearance configuration. The implementation will touch the avatar API route, the ProfileAvatar component, and the AvatarStageFigure component.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Planning to unify avatar logic by adding pose and studio props directly to AvatarFigure rather than duplicating code. Alternatively, extracting ProceduralFigure into a shared file would work. This prepares the base figure for the full avatar stage system.

Duplicating pose logic in AvatarStageFigure for speed. Building AvatarStudio as a large component. Creating PhotoCropper with circular masking, scale, and drag offsets. Planning to capture the R3F canvas via Three.js screenshot for the final profile icon.

A white flash overlay will animate during the photo capture. A dedicated profile-photo settings route will be added, and the locker will link to it. Background default cosmetics are also being considered.

The studio background picker will use owned cosmetics from the background category. A default dark stage with a red spotlight will be available for free. The patch storage SQL and API photo route are being written next.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Implementing all files now. Adding the `ProfilePose` type to `types.ts`.

Disabling animation for small AvatarFigure sizes as an interim fix. Adding avatar_url and username fields to Leaderboard rows and BattleCard voters. Building AvatarStageFigure with background environments. Starting background configuration definitions.

Defining studio background palettes with floor, wall, and accent colors. Adding space and neon city variants to the schema. Setting the default pose as the free default option.

Adding wave, flex, peace, and stance poses. Arm positioning will be driven by a pose parameter inside ProceduralAvatarMesh. The procedural mesh will be extracted into a shared component for the studio scene only. AvatarFigure will remain unchanged for the locker view. Small avatars will render as static images via ProfileAvatar. ProfileHero may display the saved avatar_url as a large image or a studio-generated photo.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here's what was implemented:

## Static profile icons (no more spinning 3D)

Battles, leaderboard, marketplace, and the header now use **`ProfileAvatar`** — a static image from `profiles.avatar_url`, with a letter fallback if no photo exists.

No WebGL, no spinning, in those spots.

## Photo Studio (`/settings/profile-photo`)

A dedicated studio flow:

1. **Mini stage** — character on a lit platform with cosmetic backgrounds  
2. **Viewfinder** — camera frame overlay (REC indicator, corner brackets)  
3. **Poses** — Chill, Wave, Flex, Peace, Stance  
4. **Backgrounds** — Hype Stage (free), Deep Space, Neon City (unlock via locker/shop)  
5. **Take photo** — white flash, captures the real 3D scene with your outfit + jewelry  
6. **Crop** — drag to reposition, zoom slider, frame face/chest  
7. **Save** — confirmation → uploads to Supabase → sets global `avatar_url`

Also linked from **Settings** and **Locker** (“Open photo studio”).

## Camera framing

Studio camera is lower and tighter (face + chest, most of body still visible). Saved photos use `object-position: center 62%` so the character sits correctly in circles.

## What you need to do

1. **Run** `supabase/patch_avatars_storage.sql` in Supabase SQL Editor (creates the `avatars` bucket)  
2. **Redeploy**  
3. Go to **Settings → Profile photo studio** (or Locker → Open photo studio)  
4. Take a photo — that becomes your icon on battles, ranks, header, etc.

Users without a studio photo still see the **initial letter** fallback until they snap one. The full 3D character remains in the locker for live customization; only the **saved photo** is used as the global icon.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The header already uses ProfileAvatar. Users without a photo studio URL see initials instead.

The header avatar should appear once avatar_url is present. A fallback snapshot was considered too complex. The profile store may not refresh after a studio save because UserProvider only loads on auth changes. The studio save should update the store via setProfile.

Minors have `is_public: false`, causing their own profile pages to incorrectly display a private message. The fix distinguishes owners from visitors by comparing the current user ID to the viewed profile ID.

A new ProfileOwnerToolbar client component will provide owner-specific controls: share profile, photo booth, locker, and settings links.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here's what changed:

## Header profile photo
The top-right link already used `ProfileAvatar`; it's now **larger (36px)** with a red ring when you have a saved photo. Username hides on very small screens so the avatar stays visible.

Profile data also **refreshes when you tab back** to the site, so a new photo booth shot shows in the header without a full reload.

**Note:** The header shows your **photo booth image** (`avatar_url`), not the live 3D character. If you only customized in the locker but haven't taken a photo yet, you'll still see your initial letter until you snap one at **Settings → Photo booth**.

## Private profile bug — fixed
Your page was blocked because minors (and some accounts) have `is_public: false`. The page treated everyone the same.

Now:
- **You** always see your full profile when logged in as that user
- **Everyone else** still sees "Private profile" if you're not public

## Your profile vs others
When viewing **your own** profile you get an owner bar with:
- **Share profile** — native share on mobile, or copies link on desktop
- **Photo booth** → `/settings/profile-photo`
- **Locker** → cosmetics & outfits
- **Edit look** → appearance settings
- **Settings**

If your profile is private, you'll also see a badge and a link to **change visibility** in privacy settings.

Redeploy to get this live. If the header still shows a letter after taking a photo, run `supabase/patch_avatars_storage.sql` if you haven't already.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The `useImperativeHandle` import is also missing.

Adding `forwardRef` to the React imports. WebGL context loss handling will be added to Canvas components via `onCreated` listeners, proper disposal on unmount, and limiting concurrent WebGL contexts. Similar fixes will be applied to AvatarFigure and other Canvas components.

Dynamic loading and navigation on the photo studio page cause WebGL contexts to accumulate. Preventing default on `webglcontextlost` allows recovery, but the root issue is too many active contexts. Cleanup on unmount and limiting concurrent contexts should resolve it.

Switching between the locker and profile photo pages leaves the Avatar3D canvas alive because the dynamic import delays cleanup. Forcing a canvas remount with a `key` prop should prevent stale WebGL contexts from accumulating.

Stabilizing the `GlBridge` `onReady` callback with `useCallback` prevents infinite re-renders caused by a new function reference on every render. The missing `forwardRef` import would crash the profile photo page entirely, since that page dynamically loads `AvatarStudio`, which renders `AvatarStageCanvas`. `Avatar3D` is less affected because the locker only ever shows one canvas.

A dedicated `WebGLCleanup` wrapper component will explicitly dispose the WebGL context on unmount. It attaches a `webglcontextlost` listener that prevents default behavior, then removes the listener and calls `gl.dispose()` in its cleanup function.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Fixed. The crash was a missing import in `AvatarStageCanvas.tsx` — it used `forwardRef` and `useImperativeHandle` without importing them from React, which broke the photo booth page entirely.

**What was fixed:**
1. **`forwardRef is not defined`** — added proper React imports (`forwardRef`, `useImperativeHandle`, `useCallback`)
2. **WebGL context lost** — added context-loss guards on all 3D canvases so the browser can recover instead of hard-crashing, plus capped `dpr` to reduce GPU pressure when multiple scenes load (locker + photo booth)

**Redeploy** and try `/settings/profile-photo` again — the photo booth should load without that error.

If you still see `Context Lost` after switching tabs quickly, a refresh is normal; it shouldn't crash the app anymore. Avoid having locker and photo booth open in two tabs at once if your GPU is limited.

---

Here’s what changed:

## Locker
- Removed the **Edit appearance** block (that’s at `/settings/avatar` now).
- Added a small link in the profile card: **“edit skin, hair & body”** → settings.

## Click to unequip
- Click an **already selected** item again → unequips that slot in the preview.
- Selected owned items show an **“Equipped”** badge; try-ons still show **“Try-on”**.

## Clear selection (under Save)
When you have unsaved changes, links appear below **Save outfit**:

- **Clear all changes** — confirmation → reverts preview to your last saved outfit.
- **Remove try-ons only** — confirmation → strips locked preview items; owned picks stay.

Both use confirmation dialogs so nothing clears by accident. Redeploy to get it live.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Consolidating all fixes into ProceduralBody.tsx. AvatarFigure will be refactored to use it with an optional rotation animation wrapper.

Adding shoulder pads at the top of the torso so arms attach properly. Rebuilding arms as hierarchical groups with pivots at the shoulders for pose rotation. Hands will sit at the end of each arm.

Unifying the head into one component with nose, mouth, and ears. Eyes will sit in the upper third of the face. Hair will be repositioned to cover the top of the head without overlapping the eyes.

Long hair will sit on a top cap with side strands trailing below the ears, keeping them off the face. Precise head metrics are being defined: eyes at 12% above center, nose at 5% below, mouth at 22% below, and ears at the sides. The hair cap will overlap slightly at the top to fully cover the head.

The hair cap will sit just above the head top with slight width and depth overhang. The torso is placed at Y=0.95 with height adjusted per gender. Shoulders will be spheres at the top of the torso, offset slightly from the body center.

The arm geometry uses a three-part hierarchy: upper arm, forearm, and hand, all nested inside a group pivoted at the shoulder. armPose now returns only rotation, while position is handled by the shoulder anchor.</details></think>

Consolidating avatar geometry into `ProceduralBody` with proper shoulders, face features, hair placement, and hands — then wiring `AvatarFigure` to use it.

Updated the character mesh in one shared `ProceduralBody` used by the locker, photo booth, and everywhere else.

**Shoulders & arms**
- Shoulder spheres + bridge blocks on the torso so arms connect properly
- Arms pivot from the shoulder (upper arm → forearm → hand)
- Hands use skin color, separate from the shirt

**Face**
- Eyes placed in the upper face (no longer overlapping hair)
- Nose, mouth, and ears on both sides
- Earrings sit at ear height

**Hair**
- Sits on top of the head as a cap (width/depth overhang)
- Short/buzz styles stay off the face
- Long hair runs down the sides/back, not over the eyes
- Ponytail attaches from the back of the cap

**Also**
- `AvatarFigure` now uses the same `ProceduralBody` so locker and photo booth match
- Hats sit above the hair cap

Redeploy and check locker or photo booth — the body should look more connected and complete.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Creating a confirmation prompt for unsaved changes on important site-wide edits.

Evaluating Zustand store and provider patterns for the unsaved changes guard. Considering `beforeunload` for browser refreshes and router event listeners for Next.js in-app navigation.

App Router makes route-change interception difficult. The plan is a Zustand store with `beforeunload` and a document-level click listener on anchor tags to block navigation while dirty. Programmatic pushes will use a guarded method.

The implementation will use a capture-phase click listener on anchor tags to intercept navigation and block it with a confirmation dialog. A Zustand store named `useUnsavedChangesStore.ts` will track the dirty state and confirmation message. The guard will be a client component placed in the root layout.

Identified specific components that will trigger the unsaved changes guard. CosmeticsLocker will watch for preview changes and set a dirty flag with a confirmation message. AvatarBuilder, AvatarStudio crop flow, onboarding step two, and photo cropper will also register unsaved work. Each component will call the store's setDirty method when its data changes.

AvatarBuilder will compare its current configuration against the initial state to detect unsaved changes.

The homepage mascot will be a custom 3D character built with React Three Fiber. It will feature a stylized appearance with flame eyes, chunky proportions, and HypeRank branding colors. This mascot will serve as a distinct brand identity separate from the user's procedural avatar.

The mascot will replace the floating logo on the homepage hero. A smaller `HypeMascotMini` variant may be exported for the header logo later. Running React Three Fiber on every page would be too heavy, so the full animated mascot stays homepage-only.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

_**Agent (default)**_

Here's what shipped:

## Unsaved changes guard (site-wide)
When you're editing something important, leaving triggers a confirmation:
- **Browser tab close / refresh** — native "leave site?" prompt
- **In-app link clicks** — `confirm()` before navigating away

Active on:
- **Locker** — unsaved outfit preview
- **Avatar appearance** editor — unsaved look changes
- **Photo booth** — captured/cropped photo not saved yet
- **Onboarding step 2** — character builder before finish

## Battle vote fix
The API already blocked double votes; the UI didn't know you'd voted. On return to a battle page it now:
- Loads your existing vote from the server
- Shows **"You voted for [trend]"** instead of vote buttons
- Handles 409 if you somehow hit vote again

## HypeRank mascot (homepage)
Replaced the floating logo with **Hype**, a 3D mascot:
- Orange flame hair, red hoodie, flex pose
- Flame ring + chest badge
- Slow spin + float animation
- Same character system as player avatars, with fixed brand colors

The `HypeMascot` component supports `hero`, `md`, and `sm` sizes so you can drop it on login, empty states, etc. later.

Redeploy to get all of this live.

---

_**User**_

when u hover over the credits in header show a tooltip explaing it briefly.. add new feature where peopel can make "posts" about their idea for the next hype thing aka lke auditions for the todays pick and the one that gets the most votes becomes the thing for the day, it cant be the same thing again tho in a week so it gets taken out the "pool" and so people can post thoseand others vote on it, people can basicaly go viral sorta thing.. itll say who made it etc, how many votes, what not, then add a featured section on the homepage,, overall add more things on the homepage, and make it flow better since its now an app so the homescreen needs to have more to do and flow more like an app instead of seperate sectonsn all seperated ( lookswise )

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Designing the Hype Auditions feature for user-submitted ideas.

Mapping out the database schema for the pitch feature. One table will store community submissions with vote counts and status tracking. Another will record individual votes. The voting system will allow users to support multiple favorite pitches rather than limiting them to a single daily pick.

Settled on one vote per user per pitch, similar to Reddit upvotes. Added a seven-day cooldown for winning pitches to prevent duplicate submissions.

Planning the daily cron logic to prioritize winning pitches over random trends. The promotion flow will create a trend from the winning submission, mark it as won, and enforce the seven-day cooldown. Considering a ban table to track inactive pitch keys.

Pitches accumulate votes throughout the day. At midnight the cron will select the highest-voted active pitch, exclude cooldown entries, promote it to a trend, and assign it as the daily featured drop.

The winning pitch will become the singular featured daily drop. The remaining four spots will still come from the existing random pool. The winning submission may generate a new trend if none exists yet. Drafting the SQL schema for the hype_pitches table now.

Finalizing the SQL schema. The pitches table will store vote counts, status, trend linkage, and timestamps. Votes will be tracked per user-pitch pair with a unique constraint. A separate cooldowns table will enforce the seven-day ban on repeated submissions.

Fleshed out the cooldown table with a banned_until date and a reference back to the original pitch. Mapped out the API endpoints for creating pitches, casting votes, and listing submissions. Defined the daily cron sequence: reset previous drops, promote the winning pitch as the featured daily trend, fill the remaining slots with random trends, and expire yesterday’s active pitches.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I understand the full scope. I am starting implementation.

Mapping out the technical plan. Adding schema tables and API routes for pitch submissions and voting. Updating the daily drop logic to promote winning pitches. Building new homepage components including featured sections and redesigned feed layout. Adding navigation links and tooltips.

The daily drop logic excludes pitches currently on cooldown. It selects the top active entries by vote count and picks the first valid winner.

The daily drop logic now converts winning pitches into trends. It checks for an existing trend by slug. If found, it updates that record. Otherwise it inserts a new trend with a unique slug. The winner's title and description populate the trend fields.

The winning pitch is marked as won and linked to the new trend. A seven-day cooldown is added to prevent the same pitch from being selected again for another week.

Winning creators receive 50 credits. Other active pitches from the same audition round expire automatically. Trend slugs are made unique by appending a timestamp or short ID when duplicates exist. The daily drop also reserves one featured slot for the winner and randomly selects four additional pitches while excluding the winner's trend.

A boolean flag will mark trends created from winning pitches as community picks. The homepage featured section will display the daily winner with their author profile. Indexes will be added to the hype_pitches table. Pitch submissions will be validated to require titles between 3 and 80 characters and descriptions up to 280 characters.

Pitch keys will be generated by slugifying titles. Each user may submit up to three pitches per day. Votes are limited to one per user per pitch. A small credit reward may be added for voting. The homepage will become a unified app feed with a compact hero strip.

The homepage hero strip will feature the mascot, a quick navigation bar with horizontal scroll chips, and a daily drop countdown. The main content area will use a rounded card surface with a subtle divider. Inside, the featured community pick section will sit above a horizontally scrolling daily drops feed.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

_**Agent (default)**_

Here's what shipped:

### Credits tooltip
Hover the credits pill in the header for a short explanation: earn from voting, streaks, and auditions; spend in the shop.

### Auditions (community pitches)
New **`/pitches`** page — pitch ideas, upvote others, see rankings and recent winners.

**How it works:**
- Submit a pitch (title + optional description)
- Others upvote (one vote per pitch per user)
- At midnight UTC, the daily cron promotes the top-voted pitch as **today's featured community pick**
- That idea enters a **7-day cooldown** (can't be pitched again)
- Winner gets **50 bonus credits**; creators get **1 credit** per upvote from others

### Homepage redesign
Single app-style feed card instead of separate spaced sections:
- Compact hero + quick-action chips (Vote, Auditions, Battles, Ranks, Shop)
- **Featured today** — community pick banner with author + vote count
- Horizontal **daily drop** scroll
- **Live auditions** preview with inline voting
- Battle teaser + trending feed in one continuous flow

### Nav
- Desktop header: **Auditions** link
- Mobile bottom nav: **Pitch** tab (replaced Ranks — still reachable from home chips)

### Database
Run this on your existing Supabase project:

```sql
-- supabase/patch_hype_pitches.sql
```

Then redeploy. Fresh installs already include the tables in `schema.sql`.

**Tables:** `hype_pitches`, `hype_pitch_votes`, `hype_pitch_cooldowns`  
**API:** `POST /api/pitches`, `POST /api/pitches/[id]/vote`
